title: Vader vs. Artoo & Threepio
content:
{{Top|rwm|ncl|inf}} {{Noncanon|legends=1|real=1}} {{ComicStory |image=[[File:VaderR2.jpg]] |title="Vader vs. Artoo & Threepio" |writer=[[Makomo Nakatsuka]] |penciller=[[Makomo Nakatsuka]] |inker= |letterer=[[Lucas Rivera]] {{C|English version}} |colorist= |illustrator= |cover artist= |editor=[[Rob Tokar]] |publisher=[[Tokyopop]] |release date=[[December]] [[2005]]{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20051222.html|text=Inside Tokyopop's ''Star Wars Manga''}} |published in=''[[Star Wars Manga: Silver]]'' |reprinted in= |pages=18 |timeline= |series=[[Tokyopop's Star Wars Manga|Tokyopop's ''Star Wars Manga'']] |preceded by= |followed by= }} "'''Vader vs. Artoo & Threepio'''" is an [[Infinities]] comic in ''[[Star Wars Manga: Silver]]''. The story merges the endings of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] and retells the story with [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] taking the place of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. The story mimics several of the scenes from the two films for comedic effect. ==Plot summary== A [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttle]] lands at the [[Endor shield generator bunker]]. As [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] approaches, the [[Igar/Legends|Commander]] introduces [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], two droids that the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperials]] have captured. C-3PO attempts to introduce himself, but Vader silences the droid, addressing R2-D2 instead. C-3PO tries to inform Vader that R2 is not programmed for communication, but he is ignored. As Vader leads R2 back to his shuttle, C-3PO frantically follows, not wanting to be left behind. After docking with the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], the [[Firmus Piett/Legends|Captain]] is informed that Vader and his prisoners are not to be disturbed. In his private quarters, Vader asks for R2 to join him and turn to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] so that they can bring order to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. R2 refuses Vader's request, and he turns and flees from [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]]. In response, Vader tells R2 that if he will not turn, then perhaps C-3PO will. 3PO, frightened, runs from Vader, but haphazardly smashes into several parts of the ship, finally falling near the lip of a [[Carbon-freezing chamber/Legends|carbon-freezing chamber]]. Vader joins C-3PO next to the freezing chamber and informs the droid that R2 never told him what happened to his creator. C-3PO claims that R2 told him that Vader killed his creator. Vader corrects the droid, stating that he is C-3PO's creator. C-3PO is shocked by the revelation, but he rationalizes that he would not have remembered since he had a [[Memory wipe/Legends|memory wipe]] some time earlier. Vader attempts to use [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to make C-3PO remember, but he is stopped by R2, who pulls a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] from inside his chassis and begins to [[Duel on the Executor|duel]] Vader. Using his [[Rocket booster/Legends|rocket boosters]] R2 is able to swipe Vader's lightsaber out of his hand. With a final swing, R2 removes the chest control panel on [[Darth Vader's armor/Legends|Vader's armor]]. With his armor damaged, Vader's respiratory functions begin to shut down, and he is unable to breathe. C-3PO decides that this is his time to escape, and he turns to run. However, he crashes into the side of the carbon-freezing chamber and falls backwards into Vader. Vader falls into the freezing chamber and is sealed in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]]. As the carbonite slab with Vader inside is pulled out of the chamber, both R2 and 3PO exclaim, "Cool!!" ==Continuity== {{Quote|See-threepio, Artoo never told you what happened to your creator.''"
"''He informed me sufficiently. He told me you killed him.''"
"''No... '''I am your creator!'''''"
"''No... no... That's not true. That's impossible! The odds against you being my creator are...''"
"''Search your feelings, You know it to be true.''"
"'''''Noooo!!''' You see, sir, shortly after the Jedi were wiped out, my memory was erased. So that could explain why I'm having difficulty verifying your statement.|Darth Vader and C-3PO|{{StoryCite|story=Vader vs. Artoo & Threepio|book=Star Wars Manga: Silver}}}} This story mimics several of the lines from both [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] for comedic effect. For example, the climactic revelation in ''The Empire Strikes Back'' is repeated, but instead of Vader revealing to [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] that he is his father, he reveals to C-3PO that he is his creator. The planet at the beginning of the story is not named, but as the shots and dialogue are obvious homages to ''Return of the Jedi'', the planet is meant to be [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. In addition, the commander who introduces the droids to Vader is meant to be [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Igar/Legends|Igar]], as the scene mimics the same scene from ''Return of the Jedi'' where Igar introduces Luke to Vader. Originally published in Japan, the stories from [[Tokyopop's Star Wars Manga]] were considered to be of "fuzzy" continuity by Lucasfilm.{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=980#15895005|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-11-06|time=6:03 PM}} Since then, they have been republished in English in the United Kingdom under [[Infinities]] label, which established the material in them as [[Canon|non-canonical]]. ==Media== ===Collected in=== *''[[Star Wars Manga: Silver]]'' ==Appearances== {{App |characters= *[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] *[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] *[[Igar/Legends|Igar]] *[[Firmus Piett/Legends|Firmus Piett]] *[[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] |species= *[[Human/Legends|Human]] |locations= *[[Endor/Legends|Forest moon of Endor]] **[[Endor shield generator bunker]] |vehicles= *[[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] *[[Imperial II-class Star *[[Lightsaber/Legends|Lightsaber]] *[[Memory wipe/Legends|Memory wipe]] |droids= *[[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|3PO-series protocol droid]] *[[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]] |organizations= *[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] |events= *[[Duel on the Executor|Duel on the ''Executor'']] *[[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]] {{Imo}} |miscellanea= *[[The Force/Legends|The Force]] **[[Dark side of the Force/Legends|Dark side of the Force]] }} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Star Wars Manga: Silver stories]]
